title: eriodictyon traskiae var. smithii
intro: Eriodictyon traskiae var. smithii, also known as Trask's yerba santa, is a variety of the Yerba Santa plant, a flowering shrub native to the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that aid in soothing and healing wounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and stress, improving respiratory issues, and alleviating skin irritations, due to its ability to calm and protect the body. Herbal preparations of Eriodictyon traskiae var. smithii include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or combining them with other herbs to create a topical remedy.
